steps.title: Set up constraints and minimize x to maximize y
steps.content:
Let $x$ = number of 100-pound packages and $y$ = number of 120-pound packages.

Two constraints apply:
  • At least 10 packages: $x + y \geq 10$
  • Weight limit: $100x + 120y \leq 1100$

To maximize $y$, we minimize $x$. From the first constraint, the minimum $x$ for a given $y$ is $x = 10 - y$.

steps.title: Solve for the maximum value of y
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 10 - y$ into the weight constraint:
$$100(10 - y) + 120y \leq 1100$$
$$1000 - 100y + 120y \leq 1100$$
$$1000 + 20y \leq 1100$$
$$20y \leq 100$$
$$y \leq 5$$
The maximum is $y = 5$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
